rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied directly to live on-chain Cardano data." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is presented as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism for builders that can advance Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs including MAU, monthly transactions, and TVL." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide line-item budget; Provide sustainability path. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.12; confidence 72.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional NO is supported by affirmative independent evidence of waste, duplication, excessive cost, or failed delivery — not by missing information.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund the Cardano Builder DAO as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects intended to advance Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs.
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO, with funds administered by an independent dRep DAO council/multisig before milestone-based disbursement.

Deliverables: Allocate capital to builders expected to improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL., Require funded projects to track core KPIs including monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions., Continue KPI dashboard development toward live on-chain Cardano data tracking., Use an independent administrator/multisig to validate milestone completion before the Cardano Builder DAO receives funds., Return unused or unallocated funds to the Cardano Treasury.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO is presented as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism for builders that can advance Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs including MAU, monthly transactions, and TVL.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied directly to live on-chain Cardano data.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Round 1 reportedly included 38 voting members, 27 requesting members, 20 funded companies, 83% governance participation, and 5,541,335 ADA distributed through a transparent smart contract-governed process.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Round 2 reportedly added 18 new members, funded 14 companies, and achieved 88% member participation after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
missing: Independent evidence for: The DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied directly to live on-chain Cardano data.

Independent evidence for: The proposal asserts that the DAO has created and amended governing documents, elected a board, added third-party assurance, implemented merit-based proposal review, and executed on-chain treasury withdrawals transparently.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is presented as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism for builders that can advance Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs including MAU, monthly transactions, and TVL." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied directly to live on-chain Cardano data." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
missing: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: No links to forum discussion or community feedback
Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
